Tests object coercion in JavaScript by verifying addition with a custom object.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of converting the object to a primitive value. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation `0`. The code includes an assertion that checks if the expression `1 + object` evaluates to `2`, demonstrating the use of the `valueOf` method in arithmetic operations. This script provides a narrow functionality focused on illustrating how JavaScript handles type conversion and operator overloading with custom object methods.
